column1: GITHUB — Username OSINT Data Point Map
Tab of Username_OSINT_DataPoints.xlsx — Developer platform OSINT for technical & identity correlation

column3: INVESTIGATIVE VALUE
Primary identifier — exact match to other platforms is very strong identity signal
Real name disclosed in profile — links to professional/legal identity
Reused image across platforms — pHash / face match
Self-description — often includes employer, links, NLP similarity to other bios
Direct identifier — often exposed in commit metadata even if hidden on profile
External URL in profile — leads to personal site, portfolio, other socials
Direct cross-platform link disclosed by user themselves
Employer disclosed — verify against LinkedIn for confirmation
Self-reported city/country — narrows geographic search
Codebase — reveals technical skillset, project topics, and naming conventions
Naming patterns may repeat across platforms (portfolio sites, npm, etc.)
Often contains author bio, contact info, social links not shown elsewhere
Interest mapping — reveals tech stack preferences and topics followed
Popularity / reputation indicator — flags high-profile or notable accounts
Reveals employer, open-source teams, or community affiliations
Activity pattern — daily/weekly rhythm hints at timezone and routine
Precise commit times reveal timezone with high accuracy across many data points
Social graph — overlap with known associates confirms real-world connections
Quick-shared snippets may contain personal info, configs, or leaked credentials
Curated showcase — reveals primary skill area and current focus projects
Account age — established account adds credibility; brand new = caution flag
Payment platform links (Patreon, Ko-fi) — may expose real name or PayPal email
Past commit emails sometimes differ from current — reveals old aliases or personal email
Cryptographic identifiers — rarely reused but can confirm identity across dev platforms
Not visible without access — requires account compromise or legal request
Not publicly visible unless leaked via commit
